metadata.title: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 in lumbar interbody fusion: A retrospective single-center cohort study with focus on complications
metadata.abstract: The purpose of this study was to report an independent real-life experience about the use of recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 in lumbar interbody fusion with a special focus on complications. This is a retrospective single-center cohort study between 2007 and 2013 including 277 patients treated for anterior or posterior lumbar fusion with recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2. We report the complications occurring during the 12 first postoperative months and analyze the fusion rate on X-rays. There are 58 cases (22.8%) of clinical complications. In 15 cases (5.9%), these complications were related to the use of recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2. Only one patient (0.4%) required a new intervention due to recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2. Fusion rate at 1 year was 98%. The low rate of specific complication suggests that recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 can be safe and effective in anterior and posterior interbody fusion when used with simple precautions.

spine_metadata.main_conclusion: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 can be safe and effective in anterior and posterior lumbar interbody fusion with a low rate of specific complications (5.9%) when used with simple precautions, achieving 98% fusion rate at 1 year.

pico.population: 277 patients undergoing lumbar interbody fusion (anterior or posterior approach) between 2007-2013
pico.intervention: Recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 (rh-BMP2) in interbody fusion
pico.outcome: Clinical complications, BMP2-related complications, fusion rate, reoperation rate

patient_cohorts.name: Study Population
patient_cohorts.diagnosis: Degenerative lumbar spine disease requiring interbody fusion

chunks.content: The purpose of this study was to report an independent real-life experience about the use of recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 in lumbar interbody fusion with a special focus on complications. This is a retrospective single-center cohort study between 2007 and 2013 including 277 patients treated for anterior or posterior lumbar fusion with recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2.
chunks.summary: Retrospective single-center study of 277 patients receiving rh-BMP2 in anterior or posterior lumbar interbody fusion between 2007-2013.

chunks.content: We report the complications occurring during the 12 first postoperative months and analyze the fusion rate on X-rays. There are 58 cases (22.8%) of clinical complications. In 15 cases (5.9%), these complications were related to the use of recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2. Only one patient (0.4%) required a new intervention due to recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2.
chunks.summary: Overall clinical complication rate was 22.8% (58/277), with only 5.9% (15/277) specifically related to rh-BMP2, and 0.4% (1/277) requiring reoperation.

chunks.content: Fusion rate at 1 year was 98%. The low rate of specific complication suggests that recombinant human bone morphogenetic protein 2 can be safe and effective in anterior and posterior interbody fusion when used with simple precautions.
chunks.summary: High fusion rate of 98% at 1 year with low BMP2-specific complication rate supports safety and efficacy of rh-BMP2 in lumbar interbody fusion.
